title: IR drop and its effect on timing
description: Current through the power grid's resistance lowers the local supply voltage, slowing logic in high-current regions.
body: Use when reasoning about why some blocks slow under load. Do not assume every cell sees the nominal voltage, because grid resistance and peak current create local droop. Actions: relate the local current and grid resistance to the voltage drop, identify high-droop regions, and connect droop to slower timing there. Validation signals include droop staying within budget and timing holding at the reduced local voltage. Risk boundary: severe droop pushes a block below its functional voltage, so flag any region whose droop threatens timing closure.
